skill.name: social-media-analyzer
skill.description: Analyzes social media campaign performance across platforms using engagement metrics, ROI calculations, benchmark comparisons, and optimization recommendations.
skill.summary: Analyze campaign engagement, ROI, top posts, and platform benchmarks from provided social media data.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the static weak-crypto, shell-backtick, and system-reconnaissance findings as false positives in documentation, JSON text, and Python docstrings. The skill contains local Python analytics scripts, but reviewed logic is limited to metric calculations, benchmark comparison, and recommendations with no network, filesystem write, subprocess, environment, or credential access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at these locations. Review found docstrings, prose, JSON report text, and field values; no cryptographic API, hash function, or security-sensitive algorithm is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited Python lines are module docstrings and the cited Markdown and JSON lines are descriptive text. I found no evidence of cryptographic code or weak algorithms at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Shell Backtick Execution Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at these locations. The reviewed lines use backticks as Markdown inline-code formatting or as JSON text copied from a prior report, not executable shell syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged SKILL.md lines document script filenames in Markdown. The eval_result.json lines are inert JSON strings and are not executed by the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance language. The cited text discusses marketing platform benchmarks and campaign comparison, with no commands or logic that enumerate the host system, users, processes, or network.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The phrase is business analytics guidance, not operating system reconnaissance. No evidence of host inspection or data collection appears in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Local Python Analytics Scripts
low_findings.description: The skill includes Python scripts that calculate metrics and recommendations from provided campaign data. Review found local arithmetic and benchmark logic only, with no file writes, network calls, subprocess execution, environment access, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The executable files are present and review confirms their purpose. Confidence is high because the relevant modules were inspected, but it remains a community skill with executable code.

content.value_statement: Social teams need clear campaign results from exported platform data. This skill calculates engagement, ROI, benchmarks, top posts, and recommendations from the data you provide.
actual_capabilities: Calculates engagement rate from likes, comments, shares, saves, and reach.
Computes click-through rate, cost per engagement, cost per click, estimated value, and ROI.
Ranks top posts by engagement rate or another provided metric.
Compares Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn, and TikTok metrics against built-in benchmarks.
Generates strengths, weaknesses, campaign health, and optimization recommendations.
limitations: It does not connect directly to social media APIs or fetch platform data.
ROI uses a fixed estimated value per engagement unless the code is adapted.
Benchmarks are general values and may not match every industry or audience.
Results depend on complete and accurate campaign data supplied by the user.

use_cases.title: Prepare client campaign reports
use_cases.description: Turn exported campaign metrics into engagement, ROI, benchmark, and recommendation summaries for client review.

use_cases.title: Improve paid social results
use_cases.description: Identify which posts and campaigns produced the strongest return before adjusting budget or creative direction.

use_cases.title: Compare platform performance
use_cases.description: Review performance across social platforms to decide where to focus content formats, posting schedules, and future tests.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Campaign Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the social-media-analyzer skill to summarize this campaign data. Include engagement rate, CTR, top post, and one recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: ROI Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the social-media-analyzer skill to calculate ROI, cost per engagement, and cost per click for this paid campaign data.

prompt_templates.title: Platform Benchmark Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the social-media-analyzer skill to compare these campaigns against platform benchmarks and explain which channel performed best.

prompt_templates.title: Full Optimization Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the social-media-analyzer skill to run a full performance audit with benchmark status, strengths, weaknesses, top posts, ROI, and next actions.

output_examples.input: Analyze an Instagram campaign with three posts, 500 dollars in spend, 1,521 engagements, 18,200 reach, and 430 clicks.
output: Average engagement rate is 8.36 percent, which is above the Instagram benchmark.
Click-through rate is 1.55 percent, which is above the Instagram benchmark.
Cost per engagement is 0.33 dollars and cost per click is 1.16 dollars.
Estimated ROI is 660.5 percent using the default engagement value.
Recommendation: scale the successful creative pattern and test similar audiences.

output_examples.input: Compare Facebook, Instagram, and LinkedIn campaign exports for last month.
output: The report ranks each platform by engagement rate, CTR, and ROI.
It labels each result against the built-in platform benchmark.
It identifies the strongest platform for budget growth and the weakest area to improve.
It recommends creative, targeting, or bidding changes based on the metric gaps.

output_examples.input: Find the best posts in a campaign and explain what to improve next.
output: The analysis lists the top posts by selected performance metric.
It highlights strong engagement, high ROI, or high CTR when present.
It flags low engagement, high CPC, or low ROI as improvement areas.
It provides practical actions such as improving calls to action or testing content formats.

best_practices: Provide reach, impressions, clicks, spend, and all engagement counts for each post.
Compare campaigns from the same time period and separate paid results from organic results.
Add campaign goals, audience context, and platform names so recommendations are easier to interpret.
anti_patterns: Using partial exports that omit reach, impressions, clicks, or spend.
Comparing platforms without considering their different benchmark baselines.
Treating estimated ROI as financial proof without validating conversion value and revenue data.

faq.question: What data should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide platform name, post metrics, reach, impressions, clicks, spend, and the campaign time period.

faq.question: Which platforms are covered?
faq.answer: The benchmark logic covers Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn, and TikTok.

faq.question: Does the skill connect to social media accounts?
faq.answer: No. It analyzes campaign data that you export or provide manually.

faq.question: How does it calculate engagement rate?
faq.answer: It divides total engagements by reach and converts the result to a percentage.

faq.question: How does it estimate ROI?
faq.answer: It multiplies engagements by a default value and compares that estimate with total spend.

faq.question: Can I use it for client reports?
faq.answer: Yes. It can produce metrics, benchmark status, top posts, strengths, weaknesses, and recommendations.
